title: Thurlow Harris
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:Tharris.jpg]] |name=Thurlow Harris |homeworld=[[Centares/Legends|Centares]][[Vader's Quest 1|''Vader's Quest'' 1]] |birth=c. [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]]The ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' manual states that Harris is twenty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old at the time of the game. ''Rebel Assault'' takes place at the same time as the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places in 0 BBY. |death=[[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Centares |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]{{SW|url=news/the-not-so-magnificent-seven|text=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|int=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|nolive=1}} |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents=[[Thurlow Harris's father]] |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Harris, standing by.|Thurlow Harris|audio=Harris-RA.flac|}} '''Thurlow Harris''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who served in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the early [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. The son of a [[Thurlow Harris's father|Rebel captain]], Harris spent much of his life in his [[Parent/Legends|father's]] shadow and witnessed many battles during his youth. Harris completed his Alliance training at the [[Kolaador Base|pilot training center]] on [[Kolaador]], and was one of three pilots sent from Kolaador to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] when [[Anchorhead Base]] was [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|attacked]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Harris helped to incapacitate an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of Tatooine, but his group arrived too late to prevent the destruction of [[Anchorhead/Legends|Anchorhead]] Base. Harris was later stationed at the Alliance's main base in the [[Great Temple/Legends|Great Temple]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] when it came under assault by the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. As part of [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]], Harris fought in the subsequent [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], in which Rebel forces destroyed the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]. He was captured soon after, however, when he returned to his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Centares/Legends|Centares]] to spread news of the Alliance's victory. After being [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], Harris revealed that [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] was the pilot responsible for the Death Star's destruction. He was subsequently [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Mala Mala]]. ==Biography== ===Rebel pilot=== {{Quote|There's one on my tail. I can't shake him. I can't shake him!''"
"''Stay on course, Harris! We'll lose them in the asteroid field.|Thurlow Harris and Merrick Simms|audio=HarrisSimms1-RA.flac|}} Born around [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Centares/Legends|Centares]] in the [[Maldrood sector/Legends|Maldrood sector]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]],{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} Thurlow Harris grew up in a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] controlled by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' manual His [[Thurlow Harris's father|father]] became a [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], so Harris had seen many battles by the [[Time/Legends|time]] he was twenty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old. Harris followed his father into the fledgling Rebellion early in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] and undertook training as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] at the Alliance's [[Kolaador Base|pilot training center]] on [[Kolaador]], alongside another recruit known as [[Rookie One]].{{CSWECite|II|22|Harris, Thurlow}} [[File:X-wingsTatooine-RA.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Harris, Murleen, and Rookie One approach Tatooine.]] In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Rebel agents acquired the [[Death Star plans/Legends|plans]] to the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], a [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] capable of destroying a planet. Pursued by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] entrusted the stolen plans to the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], who escaped her [[Tantive IV/Legends|ship]] as it was boarded by Imperial forces, landing on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] world of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. Realizing that the plans were no longer aboard the vessel, Vader ordered his forces to begin searching Tatooine.{{Film|IV}} When the Imperial search uncovered the Alliance's [[Anchorhead Base]], the Rebels called for assistance from Kolaador. In response to the distress call, Harris, Rookie One, and [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Ru Murleen]] were dispatched to Tatooine in [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]]. The three Rebels arrived in the [[Tatoo system/Legends|Tatoo system]] to find an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around Tatooine, coordinating the [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|attack]] below. As the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Space warfare/Legends|launched]] [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] against the incoming Rebels, Murleen led Harris and Rookie One in strafing runs to weaken its defenses. When the [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] protecting the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] fell under the Rebel assault, Rookie One fired [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] at the unprotected command tower, crippling the vessel and allowing the Rebel [[Flight group/Legends|flight group]] to proceed to the surface. As the Rebels approached [[Anchorhead/Legends|Anchorhead]] Base, it came under attack from three TIE fighters. Arriving too late to prevent the base's destruction, the Rebels pursued the TIE fighters into [[Beggar's Canyon/Legends|Beggar's Canyon]] to stop them from reporting the arrival of the [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]]. After all three enemies were eliminated, the Rebels were joined by the X-wing of Captain [[Merrick Simms]], and Murleen instructed Harris and Rookie One to accompany Simms while she attacked the Imperial supply lines. With enemy [[Imperial Army/Legends|ground forces]] [[Ground warfare/Legends|moving]] into [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]], Simms led Harris and Rookie One to the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] [[City/Legends|town]], where they engaged [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transports]] and Imperial [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|landing craft]] until the arrival of a TIE fighter group forced them to withdraw. With Harris struggling to outrun a [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptor]] as the Rebels fled into [[Realspace/Legends|space]], Simms led the two rookies into a nearby [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]], where they lost their pursuers before making a [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump to rendezvous with the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]]. ===Battle of Yavin=== {{Quote|I got him, I got him!''"
"''Cut the chatter, Blue Four.|Thurlow Harris and Merrick Simms|audio=HarrisSimms2-RA.flac|}} [[File:BlueSquadronDS-RA.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Blue Squadron approaches the Death Star.]] Harris was later assigned to the Alliance's main [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], but soon found himself in battle once more. The stolen Death Star plans were eventually delivered to the Rebel base, but the Empire dispatched the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] itself to destroy the moon. Analysis of the plans revealed a weakness in the battlestation which [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] formulated a plan to exploit; a direct proton torpedo hit on a small [[Thermal exhaust port/Legends|thermal exhaust port]] would trigger a chain reaction in the Death Star's [[Reactor|main reactor]] and destroy the battlestation. Harris was assigned to [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]], a group of four X-wings led by Simms and including Murleen and Rookie One; Harris received the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] [[Blue Four/Legends|Blue Four]]. Blue Squadron was charged with attacking the Death Star's [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] directly in an attempt to both damage it and divert attention away from [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red]] and [[Gold Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Gold]] [[Squadron/Legends|Squadrons]] while those groups [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|attacked]] the exhaust port.''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' As Blue Squadron approached the Death Star, a Star Destroyer in its support [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] launched TIE fighters to intercept them. The group [[Starfighter combat/Legends|battled]] their way through the incoming TIEs, with Harris getting at least one [[Death/Legends|kill]]. During the fight, three of the Imperial fighters were able to get on Simms' tail. Harris tried to go to his [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmate's]] aid, but he was unable to get a lock, leaving Rookie One to destroy the TIEs and save the [[Flight leader/Legends|flight leader]]. After making it past the perimeter, Blue Squadron commenced strafing runs on the Death Star's [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries and cleared the way for the Alliance forces to enter the narrow trench leading to the exhaust port. Though the initial attempts failed, Red Squadron pilot [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] finally made a direct hit on the exhaust port and destroyed the Death Star. The Rebels took heavy losses but Harris narrowly survived the battle. ===Capture=== {{Quote|I was born here and when I saw the Death Star gone, I wanted everyone to know that we'd won! That Vader was dead and… that the war was over! Except that it wasn't. And he isn't.''"
"''There's only one way war ends for a soldier.''"
"''I know. Now I know.''"
"''I'm sorry.''"
"''I'm not.|Thurlow Harris and Mala Mala|}} [[File:Rebeltorture.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Thurlow Harris being tortured.]] Following the Battle of Yavin, [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Vader became obsessed with taking revenge against the Rebel pilot responsible for the Death Star's destruction[[Vader's Quest 2|''Vader's Quest'' 2]] and hired [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] to track the pilot down. Believing Vader to have been killed during the battle, Harris thought that the Rebels would soon win the Galactic Civil War and returned to his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Centares in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] to spread news of the Alliance's victory. There he was captured by a group of bounty hunters including the [[Sephi/Legends|Sephi]] [[Mala Mala]], her droid [[Fordee]] and the [[Human/Legends|Human]] siblings [[Brazzo]] and [[Selle]]. Vader arrived on Centares soon after to oversee Harris's interrogation and, with the aid of [[Ban Papeega]] and a [[Unidentified torture droid|torture droid]], [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] Harris in the former [[Museum of the Old Republic]] until he revealed Skywalker's name before losing consciousness. Flying into a rage, Vader, who had been born Anakin Skywalker and was Luke Skywalker's father,{{Film|V}} ordered the bounty hunters killed to prevent Skywalker's name from reaching [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. Vader dispatched a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] to execute Harris, but Mala Mala intercepted and killed the [[Trooper/Legends|trooper]]. Going to Harris, the bounty hunter inquired what he had revealed that had caused Vader to turn on her partners. After he admitted betraying Skywalker and the Alliance, Mala Mala killed him with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] shot. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Hey, traitor!''"
"''Wha—what? A traitor. Right. It's what I am. A traitor.''"
"''As if you were the only one! What was it you told Vader? Must've been something bad!''"
"''A name. Never thought I'd become one. Always believed myself to be stronger. But it's not easy, you know, when… when they…''"
"''What name? Why is it so important?''"
"''Skywalker. He destroyed the Death Star. That's all I know. I… how do you go on living as a traitor?|Mala Mala and Thurlow Harris|}} Thurlow Harris was a Human [[Gender/Legends|male]] with [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and light skin. With his father serving in the Rebel Alliance, Harris was exposed to the Galactic Civil War at an early age and saw many battles during his youth. Harris chose to follow his father's example by joining the Alliance, but he struggled to escape his father's shadow. Although he became a skilled pilot, Harris lacked the confidence and mental control of more experienced pilots. Following the Alliance's victory at the Battle of Yavin, Harris was overjoyed and confident that the Rebels would soon win the war. Harris always believed he would be strong and not reveal anything if he was ever captured by the Empire, but his resolve failed when Darth Vader personally had him tortured following his capture on Centares. After revealing Skywalker's name, Harris was ashamed and could not forgive himself for betraying the Alliance. He ultimately welcomed his death when Mala Mala came to kill him. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|I'm hit! I'm hit! I can't hold it… I can't hold it!|The death of Thurlow Harris|audio=Harris2-RA.flac|}} [[File:HarrisTrench-RA.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Harris attempts a trench run at the Battle of Yavin.]] Thurlow Harris was created by [[Vincent Lee]] for the [[1993]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'', and his backstory was expanded in the game's manual. Harris is a non-player character in the game, who serves in the same squadron as the player's character, Rookie One. Harris's appearance in the game was modeled on footage of LucasArts artist [[Jon Knoles]].''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault: The Official Insider's Guide]]'' ''Rebel Assault'' depicts Harris, as part of Blue Squadron, taking part in a trench run at the Battle of Yavin which contradicts the battle as depicted in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. The trench run in the game is almost identical to that of the movie—only the persons involved are different. The game depicts Blue Squadron attempting a trench run with Simms entering first while Rookie One joins Murleen and Harris in providing cover. As they negotiate the long trench, the group is pursued by three TIE fighters, including a [[TIE Advanced x1/Legends|TIE Advanced x1]] such as [[Darth Vader's TIE Advanced/Legends|the one]] used by Darth Vader in the movie. The TIEs open fire, killing Harris and forcing Murleen to pull out, echoing the fates of [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]] and [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] in the movie. Simms finally reaches firing range but his torpedoes impact on the surface, leaving Rookie One next to make an attempt, mirroring [[Garven Dreis/Legends|Garven Dreis's]] failed attempt. In the game, it is Rookie One's shot that is depicted as destroying the battlestation, as opposed to Skywalker's. In [[December]] [[2006]], [[Leland Chee]], keeper of [[Lucasfilm]]'s [[Holocron continuity database]], commented on the [[StarWars.com]] [[StarWars.com Message Boards|Message Boards]] that Rookie One's trench run was non-[[Canon|continuity]], leaving it unclear whether the scene depicting Harris's death was canon.{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=1062#16021160|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-12-07|time=6:15 PM|quote=Rookiee One's tench run in non-continuity. There was less attention to staying within continuity for some of the older games.}} Harris received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ([[2008]]) which mentions his participation in the Battle of Yavin but did not clarify whether he survived the battle, and ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' ([[2012]]) later confirmed that Blue Squadron were assigned to attack the Death Star's superlaser while Red and Gold Squadrons attacked the trench. In [[September]] [[2013]], the [[Star Wars Blog]] article ''[[The Not-So Magnificent Seven]]'' by [[Greg Mitchell]] confirmed that Harris survived the battle. The article [[Retroactive continuity|established]] that a previously unidentified Rebel pilot whose interrogation and death soon after the Battle of Yavin were depicted in the [[1999]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] [[Vader's Quest 1|''Vader's Quest'' 1]] by [[Darko Macan]] is Harris. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' {{1st}} *[[Vader's Quest 1|''Vader's Quest'' 1]] {{Ret}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Thurlow Harris|soundcat=Audio files of Thurlow Harris}} *''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault: The Official Insider's Guide]]'' *{{InsiderCite|81|Star Wars Q&A|Ask the Master}} {{Imo}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{FactFile2013|1|YAV 1-4|The Battle of Yavin}} *{{SW|url=news/the-not-so-magnificent-seven|text=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|int=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|nolive=1}} *{{FactFile2014|1|0 ABY 45-48|The Battle of Yavin}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Thurlow Harris |pl=Thurlow Harris |pt=Thurlow Harris }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Harris, Thurlow}} [[Category:Blue Squadron personnel]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
